title: lovely long-cooked irish potatoes bake
description: a comforting, pillowy irish dish made with long-fermentation-dough and red potatoes, perfect for meal prep. it's also an ayurvedic-sattvic-friendly option.

ingredients.name: long-fermentation-dough
ingredients.notes: you can make your own or use store-bought

instructions: preheat your bamboo steamer over boiling water.
mix the long-fermentation-dough with the salt, black pepper, and garlic powder.
roll out the dough to a thickness of about 1/4 inch.
place the red potatoes on one half of the dough, leaving a 1/2 inch border around the edges.
dot the top of the potatoes with butter.
sprinkle the grated carrot and parsnip on top of the potatoes.
fold the other half of the dough over the potatoes and press the edges to seal.
place the stuffed dough in the bamboo steamer, seam-side down.
cover the steamer with a lid and cook for 2 hours, or until the dough is golden brown and the potatoes are tender.
remove the steamer from the heat and let it cool slightly before serving.
